reforms.description: The Action Plan for economic structural reform was adopted by the government in May 1997, aiming to boost new business activities in 15 key areas through 1,000 specific measures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan is designed to stimulate new business activities, which can enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Action Plan was adopted and is being coordinated by the Ministry of International Trade and Industry.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Action Plan represents a comprehensive approach to structural reform, targeting multiple sectors to enhance innovation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The Action Plan for economic structural reform was adopted by the government in May 1997 and is being co-ordinated by the Ministry of International Trade and Industry.

reforms.description: The government has initiated a structural reform programme aimed at liberalising economic activity, which includes a variety of measures such as the financial 'Big Bang' and the establishment of financial holding companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms are expected to enhance competition and efficiency in the financial sector, which can lead to increased investment and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The implementation phase of the structural reform programme is now underway.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in Japan's economic policy, aiming to address long-standing inefficiencies and stimulate growth through comprehensive structural changes.
reforms.source_quote: The implementation phase is now under way in a variety of domains, most notably the financial 'Big Bang'...

reforms.description: The government has initiated reforms to enhance the nation's innovative capacity by improving the flow of information and researchers among government research laboratories, universities, and businesses, while also raising the efficiency of public funding through competitive budgetary allocations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing innovative capacity is expected to lead to increased productivity and economic growth through improved R&D collaboration and funding efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken initiatives to enhance the nation's innovative capacity.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at revitalizing Japan's innovation ecosystem, which is crucial for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The government has taken initiatives to enhance the nation's innovative capacity: the hitherto compartmentalised R&D system is to be improved through increased flows of information and researchers among the government research laboratories, universities and businesses.

reforms.description: The Japanese government launched a new three-year Deregulation Action Plan aimed at reviewing and reforming approximately 12,000 permitting and licensing regulations to enhance economic efficiency and competition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing regulatory burdens, the plan aims to foster competition and innovation, which can lead to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new three-year plan was launched and is being implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in regulatory policy aimed at enhancing competition and efficiency across multiple sectors, which is crucial for Japan's economic adaptation to global market dynamics.
reforms.source_quote: A new three-year Plan was launched... the new committee is also to undertake 'horizontal' work, which is applicable to many areas.

reforms.description: The government announced a new law to eliminate the use of supply/demand adjustment mechanisms for large retail stores, requiring local governments to apply transparent criteria for store approvals.
reforms.package_name: Large Scale Retail Store Law Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing barriers to entry for new retail chains, the reform is expected to enhance competition and consumer choice, potentially leading to increased economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: A new law will be implemented by June 2000.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in retail policy, aimed at fostering competition and innovation in the retail sector, which is crucial for economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: A new law will be implemented by June 2000; it will eliminate the use of supply/demand adjustment mechanisms and will require local governments to apply transparent criteria.

reforms.description: The Ministry of Justice announced plans to revise the Composition Law to allow firms to seek settlements with creditors before submitting a rehabilitation plan, facilitating smoother bankruptcy processes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By streamlining bankruptcy procedures, the reform aims to reduce exit costs for failing firms, thereby enabling more efficient resource allocation and encouraging new market entries.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry of Justice has just announced that it will present revisions to the Composition Law in 1999.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses significant inefficiencies in the bankruptcy system, which can enhance the overall business environment and support economic recovery.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of Justice has just announced that it will present revisions to the Composition Law in 1999.

reforms.description: The Science Basic Plan is being implemented to promote an open and competitive R&D system, emphasizing the importance of basic research and improving the efficiency of public spending on technology.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing basic research is expected to lead to innovations that can drive economic growth and improve productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The Science Basic Plan is being implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The implementation of the Science Basic Plan represents a significant structural change in Japan's approach to R&D funding, aiming to enhance the overall efficiency and effectiveness of public research efforts.
reforms.source_quote: The Science Basic Plan is being implemented to promote open and competitive R&D system.

reforms.description: The Japanese government has adopted measures to promote the establishment and growth of venture companies by improving the provision of private risk capital, including tax incentives and easing investment restrictions.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the supply of risk capital is expected to stimulate new business formation and innovation, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has adopted a number of measures to promote the establishment and growth of venture companies.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift in policy aimed at fostering a vibrant startup ecosystem, which is crucial for long-term economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The government has adopted a number of measures to promote the establishment and growth of venture companies largely by improving the provision of private risk capital.

reforms.description: The Science and Technology Basic Plan aims to increase government investment in R&D significantly, with a target of 17 trillion yen from FY 1996 to FY 2000, to enhance Japan's R&D capabilities and boost basic research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased public investment in R&D is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was decided in July 1996 and outlines significant government investment in R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in Japan's approach to R&D funding, aiming to align investment levels with those of other developed countries and enhance overall innovation capacity.
reforms.source_quote: The Plan envisages a large increase in government investment in R&D (17 trillion yen in total from FY 1996 to FY 2000).

reforms.description: The government introduced a law to promote technology transfer from universities to industry, which aims to enhance collaboration and commercialization of research outcomes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating technology transfer is expected to enhance innovation and economic growth by bridging the gap between academic research and industry application.
reforms.status_evidence: A law to promote technology transfer from universities to industry, which passed the Diet in May 1998.
reforms.importance_rationale: This law represents a significant step towards enhancing collaboration between academia and industry, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: A law to promote technology transfer from universities to industry, which passed the Diet in May 1998.

reforms.description: The government has initiated measures to improve the R&D system by increasing information and researcher flows among government research laboratories, universities, and businesses, while enhancing the efficiency of R&D funding through competitive budgetary allocations and strict evaluations of publicly funded projects.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the efficiency of R&D funding and improving collaboration among research entities is expected to boost innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has taken measures to facilitate the desirable changes.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the R&D funding system, aiming to enhance collaboration and efficiency, which is crucial for fostering innovation in Japan.
reforms.source_quote: The government has readied the legal and institutional infrastructure for a potentially huge increase in the supply of risk capital, including the easing of restrictions on investment in unlisted shares by pension funds.

reforms.description: The government has revised patent-related laws to strengthen the protection of innovators' intellectual property rights and promote the use of dormant patents, which are valuable to middle-sized companies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening intellectual property rights encourages innovation and commercialization of new ideas, which can lead to increased economic activity.
reforms.status_evidence: The patent-related laws have been revised to strengthen the protection of innovators’ intellectual property rights.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform significantly enhances the legal framework for intellectual property, which is essential for fostering innovation and protecting the interests of inventors.
reforms.source_quote: The patent-related laws have been revised to strengthen the protection of innovators’ intellectual property rights.

reforms.description: The government facilitated the diffusion of advanced imported technologies and encouraged R&D investment through the tax system, helping companies obtain favorable terms for technology importation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Encouraging R&D investment and technology adoption can enhance productivity and innovation in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government played a role in facilitating technology diffusion and R&D investment.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant policy change aimed at enhancing R&D investment and technology adoption, which is crucial for economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The role of the government in the catching-up process was mainly to facilitate the diffusion of advanced imported technologies and encourage R&D investment through the tax system.
